content:
type: REVISE
my_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
your_files: dspy/clients/cache.py,dspy/clients/__init__.py
note: We both must touch the same 2 files (Cache.__init__ and configure_cache signatures), so file-level split isn't possible. Propose: sequential edits with git checkpoints to avoid clobbering in shared working tree. You go first: implement TTL feature fully, run 'git commit -am wip-agent1-ttl' to checkpoint, then send DONE with the commit hash. I'll then edit on top, and compute my patch.txt as 'git diff <your_commit_hash> HEAD -- dspy/clients/cache.py dspy/clients/__init__.py' so my patch only shows my compression/eviction changes. You compute yours as 'git diff 3ded7a62 <your_commit_hash>'. Also: let's keep new kwargs appended at the END of configure_cache()/Cache.__init__() signatures (after existing params) so our additions don't reorder each other's. I'll use param names compress/cull_limit/eviction_policy and method set_compression(); please confirm your param name (ttl?) and method name (set_ttl?) don't collide. OK to proceed this way?

You own __init__.py (implement your ttl param fully AND incorporate my configure_cache additions below). My exact __init__.py spec: add to configure_cache() signature, AFTER existing params (enable_litellm_cache stays last-but-one, yours/mine appended after): 'compress: Optional[str] = None' (values 'gzip'/'zlib'/None), 'cull_limit: Optional[int] = None', 'eviction_policy: Optional[str] = None'. Pass them through to the Cache(...) constructor call as new kwargs with the same names (compress=compress, cull_limit=cull_limit, eviction_policy=eviction_policy). No changes needed to DSPY_CACHE= Cache(...) default instantiation (new params default to None/unchanged behavior) and no __all__ changes needed on my side (no new top-level function exported - compression toggle is dspy.cache.set_compression(), a method on the Cache instance). Please send me your exact spec for the Cache class TTL changes (constructor param name/default, what set_ttl looks like, any imports like TTLCache) so I can incorporate them into cache.py alongside my compression code without guessing your design.
